growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: engqvist.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

up.method: identified as substrate (chemostat gas feed ~60% CO/40% Ar); numeric specific uptake flux reported previously in ref 18 / Table S5, not given numerically in this paper
listed as a measured uptake constraint for FBA; numeric value not in-text

sec.method: growth by-product; 1/3-2/3 of fixed carbon channeled to acetate+ethanol; numeric specific flux reported previously (ref 18/Table S5)
growth by-product; ethanol flux increases with increasing H2 supply; numeric flux in ref 18/Table S5
minor by-product; numeric flux in ref 18/Table S5
by-product of CO oxidation; CO2 flux decreases with increasing H2 supply; numeric flux in ref 18/Table S5

growth_records.cite: Valgepea et al. Absolute Proteome Quantification in the Gas-Fermenting Acetogen Clostridium autoethanogenum. mSystems (2022). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00026-22. [PMC9040625]
growth_records.snippet: four biological replicate chemostat cultures of C. autoethanogenum strain DSM 19630 were grown on a chemically defined medium (without yeast extract) on CO (~60% CO and 40% Ar)...The bioreactors were maintained at 37°C, pH 5, and a dilution
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady state: growth_rate=dilution_rate. Reported mu ~0.04/h and D ~1 day-1. D per h = 1/24 = 0.0417/h (consistent with reported ~0.04/h). doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.693/0.04 = 17.33 h. Medium composition not enumerated in-text (defined medium without yeast extract, detailed in refs 18,19); medium_composition_stated=false. Uptake (CO, cysteine) and secretion (acetate, ethanol, 2,3-butanediol, CO2) fluxes are qualitatively named but numeric mmol/gDCW/h values were reported previously (ref 18, Table S5 XLSX) and are not present in this paper's text.

up.method: syngas feed ~50% CO; numeric specific uptake flux in ref 18/Table S5
syngas feed ~20% H2; numeric flux in ref 18/Table S5
syngas feed ~20% CO2 (co-substrate for WLP); net direction/flux in ref 18/Table S5
measured uptake constraint; numeric value not in-text

sec.method: growth by-product; numeric flux in ref 18/Table S5
growth by-product; numeric flux in ref 18/Table S5
minor by-product; numeric flux in ref 18/Table S5

growth_records.cite: Valgepea et al. Absolute Proteome Quantification in the Gas-Fermenting Acetogen Clostridium autoethanogenum. mSystems (2022). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00026-22. [PMC9040625]
growth_records.snippet: grown on a chemically defined medium (without yeast extract) on...syngas (~50% CO, 20% H2, 20% CO2, and 10% N2/Ar)...maintained at 37C, pH 5, and a dilution rate of ~1 day-1 (specific growth rate of ~0.04 h-1).
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady state: mu=D. Reported mu ~0.04/h, D ~1 day-1 = 0.0417/h. doubling_time = ln2/0.04 = 17.33 h. Syngas contains CO2 as co-substrate so CO2 can be consumed via Wood-Ljungdahl pathway. Medium composition not stated in-text (medium_composition_stated=false). Specific gas uptake (CO, H2, CO2) and product secretion (acetate, ethanol, 2,3-butanediol) fluxes reported previously (ref 18, Table S5), not numerically in this paper.

up.method: high-H2 CO feed ~15% CO; numeric flux in ref 18/Table S5
high-H2 CO feed ~45% H2; numeric flux in ref 18/Table S5
measured uptake constraint; numeric value not in-text

sec.method: growth by-product; ethanol flux significantly INCREASED with increasing H2 supply (highest under high-H2 CO); numeric flux in ref 18/Table S5
growth by-product; numeric flux in ref 18/Table S5
minor by-product; numeric flux in ref 18/Table S5
by-product; CO2 flux significantly DECREASED with increasing H2 supply; numeric flux in ref 18/Table S5

growth_records.cite: Valgepea et al. Absolute Proteome Quantification in the Gas-Fermenting Acetogen Clostridium autoethanogenum. mSystems (2022). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00026-22. [PMC9040625]
growth_records.snippet: CO+H2, termed high-H2CO (~15% CO, 45% H2, and 40% Ar)...maintained at 37C, pH 5, and a dilution rate of ~1 day-1 (specific growth rate of ~0.04 h-1). ...most striking were the significantly increased and decreased carbon fluxes to ethanol a
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady state: mu=D. Reported mu ~0.04/h; D ~1 day-1 = 0.0417/h; doubling_time = ln2/0.04 = 17.33 h. Directional trend explicit in-text: with increasing H2 supply, ethanol flux increased and CO2 flux decreased across the three gas mixtures. Medium composition not enumerated in-text (medium_composition_stated=false). Numeric specific fluxes (CO, H2, cysteine uptake; acetate, ethanol, 2,3-butanediol, CO2 secretion) reported previously (ref 18, Table S5).

growth_records.cite: Ghadermazi et al. Metabolic Engineering Interventions for Sustainable 2,3-Butanediol Production in Gas-Fermenting Clostridium autoethanogenum. mSystems (2022). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01111-21. [PMC9040633]
growth_records.snippet: Syngas | LBC | 50% CO, 20% CO2, 20% H2, 10% Ar | qCO -18.8 | qCO2 5.65 | qH2 -12.6 | qAcet 6.35 | qEtOH 1.2 | qBDO 0 | mu 0.04 (Table 1, refs 24,44)
growth_records.notes: Experimental gas-fermentation validation data compiled in Table 1 of this modeling paper (original source refs 24,44). Rates in mmol/gCDW/h; negative values = uptake (CO, H2), positive = secretion (CO2, acetate, ethanol, BDO). mu=0.04 h^-1 reported directly. doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.04 = 17.33 h. LBC = low biomass concentration continuous culture; culture_mode inferred chemostat because LBC/MBC/HBC series share identical mu=0.04 at steady state (D=mu). Temperature/pH not stated in this paper.

growth_records.cite: Ghadermazi et al. Metabolic Engineering Interventions for Sustainable 2,3-Butanediol Production in Gas-Fermenting Clostridium autoethanogenum. mSystems (2022). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01111-21. [PMC9040633]
growth_records.snippet: Syngas | MBC | 50% CO, 20% CO2, 20% H2, 10% Ar | qCO -24.6 | qCO2 8.7 | qH2 -12.5 | qAcet 5.2 | qEtOH 2.5 | qBDO 0.01 | mu 0.04 (Table 1, refs 24,44)
growth_records.notes: Table 1 validation data (refs 24,44). mmol/gCDW/h; negative=uptake, positive=secretion. mu=0.04 h^-1 reported; doubling=ln2/0.04=17.33 h. MBC=medium biomass concentration steady-state continuous culture (chemostat inferred). Temperature/pH not stated.

growth_records.cite: Ghadermazi et al. Metabolic Engineering Interventions for Sustainable 2,3-Butanediol Production in Gas-Fermenting Clostridium autoethanogenum. mSystems (2022). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01111-21. [PMC9040633]
growth_records.snippet: Syngas | HBC | 50% CO, 20% CO2, 20% H2, 10% Ar | qCO -30.6 | qCO2 12.55 | qH2 -11.9 | qAcet 4.05 | qEtOH 3.75 | qBDO 0.09 | mu 0.04 (Table 1, refs 24,44)
growth_records.notes: Table 1 validation data (refs 24,44). mmol/gCDW/h; negative=uptake, positive=secretion. mu=0.04 h^-1 reported; doubling=ln2/0.04=17.33 h. HBC=high biomass concentration steady-state continuous culture (chemostat inferred). Temperature/pH not stated.

growth_records.cite: Ghadermazi et al. Metabolic Engineering Interventions for Sustainable 2,3-Butanediol Production in Gas-Fermenting Clostridium autoethanogenum. mSystems (2022). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01111-21. [PMC9040633]
growth_records.snippet: Syngas | LBC | 50% CO, 20% CO2, 20% H2, 10% Ar | qCO -18,792 | qCO2 5,625 | qH2 -12,583 | qAcet 6,333 | qEtOH 1.25 | qBDO 0 | mu 0.04 (Table 1, ref 40)
growth_records.notes: Table 1 validation data (ref 40); same LBC syngas condition as row 1 but reported from a second source. Original table used European decimal commas: -18,792 = -18.792; 5,625 = 5.625; -12,583 = -12.583; 6,333 = 6.333 mmol/gCDW/h (confirmed by consistency with ref 24/44 row values -18.8/5.65/-12.6/6.35). negative=uptake, positive=secretion. mu=0.04 h^-1 reported; doubling=ln2/0.04=17.33 h. Chemostat inferred (LBC steady state). Temperature/pH not stated.

growth_records.cite: Ghadermazi et al. Metabolic Engineering Interventions for Sustainable 2,3-Butanediol Production in Gas-Fermenting Clostridium autoethanogenum. mSystems (2022). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01111-21. [PMC9040633]
growth_records.snippet: High-H2 CO | LBC | 15% CO, 45% H2, 40% Ar | qCO -20,042 | qCO2 2,125 | qH2 -33,042 | qAcet 1,083 | qEtOH 9,042 | qBDO 0 | mu 0.04 (Table 1, ref 40)
growth_records.notes: Table 1 validation data (ref 40). European decimal commas in original: -20,042 = -20.042; 2,125 = 2.125; -33,042 = -33.042; 1,083 = 1.083; 9,042 = 9.042 mmol/gCDW/h. High H2 substrate drives high H2 uptake (-33.042) and shifts product spectrum toward ethanol (9.042) with low acetate. negative=uptake, positive=secretion. mu=0.04 h^-1 reported; doubling=ln2/0.04=17.33 h. Chemostat inferred. Temperature/pH not stated.

sec.method: reported (qH2 = +0.5, Table 1 ref 40; positive = H2 produced, no H2 in feed)

growth_records.cite: Ghadermazi et al. Metabolic Engineering Interventions for Sustainable 2,3-Butanediol Production in Gas-Fermenting Clostridium autoethanogenum. mSystems (2022). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01111-21. [PMC9040633]
growth_records.snippet: CO | HBC | 60% CO, 40% Ar | qCO -31 | qCO2 21 | qH2 0.5 | qAcet 3.3 | qEtOH 2.5 | qBDO 0.03 | mu 0.04 (Table 1, ref 40)
growth_records.notes: Table 1 validation data (ref 40). CO-only substrate (no H2 fed): qH2 = +0.5 is positive, i.e., net H2 production, so classified as secretion. High CO2 loss (21 mmol/gCDW/h) reflects ~2/3 carbon lost to CO2 on CO-only substrate (11 CO + 5 H2O -> C4H10O2 + 7 CO2). mmol/gCDW/h; negative CO = uptake. mu=0.04 h^-1 reported; doubling=ln2/0.04=17.33 h. Chemostat inferred (HBC steady state). Temperature/pH not stated.

growth_records.cite: Ghadermazi et al. Metabolic Engineering Interventions for Sustainable 2,3-Butanediol Production in Gas-Fermenting Clostridium autoethanogenum. mSystems (2022). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01111-21. [PMC9040633]
growth_records.snippet: Syngas | HBC | 50% CO, 20% CO2, 20% H2, 10% Ar | qCO -30 | qCO2 12 | qH2 -11 | qAcet 4 | qEtOH 3.75 | qBDO 0.06 | mu 0.04 (Table 1, ref 40)
growth_records.notes: Table 1 validation data (ref 40); syngas HBC condition (comparable to ref 24/44 HBC row). mmol/gCDW/h; negative=uptake (CO, H2), positive=secretion. mu=0.04 h^-1 reported; doubling=ln2/0.04=17.33 h. Chemostat inferred (HBC steady state). Temperature/pH not stated.

growth_records.cite: Ghadermazi et al. Metabolic Engineering Interventions for Sustainable 2,3-Butanediol Production in Gas-Fermenting Clostridium autoethanogenum. mSystems (2022). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01111-21. [PMC9040633]
growth_records.snippet: High-H2 CO | HBC | 15% CO, 45% H2, 40% Ar | qCO -20 | qCO2 4 | qH2 -29 | qAcet 1.9 | qEtOH 7.88 | qBDO 0 | mu 0.04 (Table 1, ref 40)
growth_records.notes: Table 1 validation data (ref 40). High-H2 substrate: strong H2 uptake (-29) shifts products toward ethanol (7.88) and reduces CO2 loss (4). mmol/gCDW/h; negative=uptake, positive=secretion. mu=0.04 h^-1 reported; doubling=ln2/0.04=17.33 h. Chemostat inferred (HBC steady state). Temperature/pH not stated.

sec.method: linear regression product vs biomass, R2>=0.92

growth_records.cite: Nwaokorie et al. Deletion of genes linked to the C1-fixing gene cluster affects growth, by-products, and proteome of Clostridium autoethanogenum. Frontiers in Bioengineering and Biotechnology (2023). DOI:10.3389/fbioe.2023.1167892. [PMC10230548]
growth_records.snippet: the maximum specific growth rate (µmax)... LAbrini base strain showed µmax values of 0.10 ± 0.001 on fructose and 0.08 ± 0.001 on syngas
growth_records.notes: µmax(syngas,rich)=0.08±0.001 h-1 (reported). td=ln2/0.08=8.66 h. Yields are production yields mmol/gDCW (not per-time rates): acetate 92±7, ethanol 102±13, 2,3-BDO 7±1. Acetate/ethanol ~1.

growth_records.cite: Nwaokorie et al. Deletion of genes linked to the C1-fixing gene cluster affects growth, by-products, and proteome of Clostridium autoethanogenum. Frontiers in Bioengineering and Biotechnology (2023). DOI:10.3389/fbioe.2023.1167892. [PMC10230548]
growth_records.snippet: the Δhp strain showed a >5-fold higher acetate yield (550 ± 45 mmol/gDCW) relative to LAbrini (92 ± 7 mmol/gDCW) during growth in rich medium
growth_records.notes: µmax(syngas,rich)=0.05±0.001 h-1 (37% lower than LAbrini). td=13.86 h. Yields mmol/gDCW: acetate 550±45, ethanol 96±5, 2,3-BDO 10±1. Ace/EtOH ~5.

growth_records.cite: Nwaokorie et al. Deletion of genes linked to the C1-fixing gene cluster affects growth, by-products, and proteome of Clostridium autoethanogenum. Frontiers in Bioengineering and Biotechnology (2023). DOI:10.3389/fbioe.2023.1167892. [PMC10230548]
growth_records.snippet: the Δnbp strain showed significantly different product yields... 41% higher acetate (130 ± 0.2 vs. 92 ± 7 mmol/gDCW), 44% higher 2,3-BDO (10 ± 1 vs. 7 ± 1 mmol/gDCW), and 33% lower ethanol (65 ± 8 vs. 96 ± 6 mmol/gDCW)
growth_records.notes: µmax(syngas,rich)=0.06±0.001 h-1 (26% lower than LAbrini). td=11.55 h. Yields mmol/gDCW: acetate 130±0.2, ethanol 65±8, 2,3-BDO 10±1. Ace/EtOH ~2.

growth_records.snippet: the specific CO uptake rates (qCO; mmol/gDCW/day) were slightly lower for both deletion strains compared to LAbrini (873 ± 19): by 8% for the Δhp strain (805 ± 19) and by 5% for the Δnbp strain (830 ± 15)
growth_records.notes: Chemostat D=1 day-1 = 1/24 = 0.0417 h-1; at steady state µ=D=0.0417 h-1 (calculated), td=ln2/0.0417=16.6 h. qCO uptake=873±(19/19/15) mmol/gDCW/day (reported for LAbrini/Δhp/Δnbp=873/805/830). qH2, qCO2 no significant strain difference (numeric only in Fig 7B). qacetate/qEtOH lower in deletion strains (Fig 7A, values only graphical). Acetate/ethanol Cmol ratio=1.3. 37°C, pH 5.
